message:
fix(cli/contacts): route project resolution through context::resolve_project for synthetic-id tolerance

Extend the orphaned-project-metadata tolerance work landed earlier
in this batch (see "fix(cli,server): tolerate orphaned project
metadata + harden mail status / projects adopt / TUI compose paths")
to the four contacts-command call sites in handle_contacts_with_conn
that were still doing open-coded `SELECT id FROM projects WHERE
slug = ?` lookups.  Those sites failed closed with
`CliError::InvalidArgument("project not found: ...")` whenever the
projects row was missing, even though the agents and agent_links
rows for that project were perfectly intact — blocking operators
from managing contacts on projects whose metadata had been wiped
by an earlier broken cleanup.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs

=== The migration ===

Replace the four instances of:
    let proj_rows = conn
        .query_sync(
            "SELECT id FROM projects WHERE slug = ?",
            &[sqlmodel_core::Value::Text(project_key.clone())],
        )
        .map_err(|e| CliError::Other(format!("query failed: {e}")))?;
    let project_id: i64 = proj_rows
        .first()
        .and_then(|r| r.get_named("id").ok())
        .ok_or_else(|| {
            CliError::InvalidArgument(format!("project not found: {project_key}"))
        })?;

with the single line:
    let project_id = crate::context::resolve_project(conn, &project_key)?.id;

resolve_project already knows how to:
- look up the projects row when it exists,
- recognize a "[unknown-project-N]" synthetic placeholder slug and
  return a synthetic Project struct with id = N so downstream agent /
  link queries that key on project_id continue to work,
- produce a consistent CliError::InvalidArgument when neither path
  resolves.

The four affected sites inside handle_contacts_with_conn are:

1. ContactsCommand::Request (contact request creation) — line ~8595.
2. ContactsCommand::Respond (accept/decline) — line ~8678.
3. ContactsCommand::ListContacts (list incoming/outgoing links) —
   line ~8734.
4. ContactsCommand::SetPolicy (update contact_policy on an agent) —
   line ~8861.

=== Why this matters ===

Before: `mail contacts request --project-key "[unknown-project-1]"
--from BlueLake --to RedFox ...` against a DB whose projects row
for id=1 had been deleted (by a crash, migration, or earlier
cleanup) would exit with "project not found: [unknown-project-1]"
even though the agent_links table could trivially handle the
(a_agent_id, b_agent_id) insert on its own.  That left operators
unable to manage contacts for projects that were otherwise in a
recoverable state — the exact scenario that the synthetic-id
placeholder exists to address.

After: the four contacts commands route through the same
context::resolve_project helper that the mail-status, mail-send,
and TUI-compose paths already use, so they tolerate the same set
of orphaned-metadata states.

=== New regression tests ===

Three new integration tests that all use the same fixture pattern:
seed a fresh DB with two agents (BlueLake, RedFox), delete the
projects row, then exercise a contacts subcommand against the
"[unknown-project-1]" synthetic slug.

- integration_contacts_request_accepts_orphaned_project_placeholder:
    runs ContactsCommand::Request, asserts Ok(), asserts the JSON
    output contains `"status"` and `"pending"`, and asserts the
    agent_links row was written with (a_agent_id=1, b_agent_id=2)
    and status="pending".

- integration_contacts_list_accepts_orphaned_project_placeholder:
    runs a ContactsCommand::Request first (same fixture), then
    deletes the projects row, then runs ContactsCommand::ListContacts
    with agent_name="BlueLake" and asserts the JSON output contains
    `"direction"`, `"outgoing"`, and the target agent name "RedFox".

- integration_contacts_policy_accepts_orphaned_project_placeholder:
    runs ContactsCommand::SetPolicy to flip the contact_policy on
    an agent and asserts the row update succeeds against the
    synthetic placeholder.

All three tests wrap the capture in stdio_capture_lock() and the
standard ftui_runtime::StdioCapture::install() harness so the
JSON-format assertions can read from stdout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
